enrichment.short_description: Open-source, typo-tolerant search engine with built-in vector and semantic search, self-hosted or via Typesense Cloud.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Typesense is an open-source search engine with typo tolerance, vector search, and a usage-based managed cloud option.

features.title: Typo tolerance
features.description: Automatically corrects misspellings in search queries.

features.title: Vector & semantic search
features.description: Matches results by meaning, not just exact keywords.

features.title: Faceting, filtering & geo-search
features.description: Narrow results by structured fields and location.

features.title: Synonyms & tunable ranking
features.description: Customize relevance rules for each use case.

features.title: Federated search
features.description: Query multiple collections in a single request.

features.title: Multi-tenant API keys
features.description: Scope search access per tenant or customer.

pros: Fully open-source and self-hostable, avoiding vendor lock-in.
Combines keyword, vector, and hybrid search in one engine.
Official SDKs for ten-plus languages including JS, Python, PHP, Ruby, and Go.
Typesense Cloud removes the need to operate infrastructure yourself.
cons: Typesense Cloud pricing is usage-based with no published flat starting price.
Self-hosting requires you to manage servers or clusters yourself.
Smaller community (25K GitHub stars) than larger incumbents like Algolia or Elasticsearch.

faqs.question: Is Typesense open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Typesense is open-source and can be self-hosted via Docker, native binaries, or RPM/DEB packages.

faqs.question: Does Typesense support vector search?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports vector and semantic search alongside traditional keyword search.

faqs.question: Can I run Typesense without paying?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting is free; Typesense Cloud is a paid, usage-based managed option.

faqs.question: What languages have official Typesense SDKs?
faqs.answer: JavaScript, Go, PHP, Clojure, Swift, Ruby, Python, Dart, .NET, and Java.

use_cases.title: Site & app search
use_cases.description: Add typo-tolerant, faceted search to websites, e-commerce catalogs, and apps.

use_cases.title: AI/RAG retrieval
use_cases.description: Use built-in vector search to power retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

use_cases.title: Multi-tenant SaaS search
use_cases.description: Isolate search per customer using multi-tenant API keys.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Typesense?
paragraphs: Typesense is an open-source search engine that adds typo tolerance, faceting, geo-search, and built-in vector/semantic search to keyword search.
It can be self-hosted via Docker or native binaries, or run as a managed service through Typesense Cloud, which bills based on cluster resources rather than record count.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Typesense is for
paragraphs: Typesense suits developers who want an Algolia-like search experience without proprietary lock-in, including teams building e-commerce search, documentation search, or AI retrieval features.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered search and retrieval platform offering neural search, personalization, and RAG tools for sites and apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Algolia offers AI-powered search, personalization, and retrieval tools for 18,000+ customers, with a free tier and paid plans.

features.title: AI Search & Retrieval
features.description: Neural search combining keyword and semantic matching.

features.title: Personalization
features.description: Customizes results and rankings per visitor.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Dashboards for search performance insights.

features.title: Agent Studio
features.description: Build and deploy AI agents on top of indexed data.

features.title: Generative Experiences
features.description: RAG-based answers surfaced within the search interface.

features.title: Pre-built integrations
features.description: Connects to platforms like Shopify, Salesforce, Adobe, and AWS.

pros: Free Build plan available with no credit card required.
Serves 18,000+ customers across 150+ countries, indicating broad production use.
SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA certifications for compliance-sensitive teams.
Named a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Search and Product Discovery for three consecutive years.
cons: Advanced AI features (NeuralSearch, AI Synonyms/Ranking) require the pricier Grow Plus tier, not the base Grow plan.
The Elevate enterprise tier requires an annual contract with custom pricing.
Pricing is usage-based, so costs scale with request and record volume rather than a flat fee.

faqs.question: Does Algolia have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Build plan is free and includes 10K search requests/month and 1M records.

faqs.question: Does Algolia support AI/semantic search?
faqs.answer: Yes, AI capabilities like NeuralSearch and Ask AI are available starting on the Grow Plus plan.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Algolia hold?
faqs.answer: SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA, among others.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for enterprise features?
faqs.answer: The Elevate enterprise tier requires an annual contract with custom pricing rather than a trial.

use_cases.title: E-commerce product discovery
use_cases.description: Power faceted, typo-tolerant product search and merchandising.

use_cases.title: Enterprise & documentation search
use_cases.description: Search internal knowledge bases or docs with AI-assisted retrieval.

use_cases.title: AI agents & RAG
use_cases.description: Build generative, agentic experiences on top of indexed content via Agent Studio.

features: Adds NeuralSearch, AI Synonyms, AI Ranking

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Algolia?
paragraphs: Algolia is an AI-powered search and retrieval platform used by 18,000+ customers to add neural search, personalization, and generative (RAG) experiences to websites and apps.
Beyond core search, it offers Agent Studio for building AI agents and an MCP server for agentic workflows, alongside enterprise-grade security certifications.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Algolia is for
paragraphs: Algolia fits e-commerce, media, SaaS, and enterprise teams that need production-grade search with a free tier to start and usage-based pricing that scales with traffic.

enrichment.short_description: Managed vector database for storing and querying embeddings to power semantic search, RAG, and AI agents at scale.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pinecone is a fully managed vector database for AI apps, with a free Starter tier and paid plans starting at $20/month.

features.title: Serverless vector database
features.description: Stores and searches billions of embeddings without capacity planning.

features.title: Hybrid search
features.description: Combines vector, keyword, and metadata filtering.

features.title: Sub-second freshness
features.description: Newly indexed data is searchable within seconds.

features.title: Built-in inference & Assistant
features.description: Generate embeddings and build RAG assistants without separate infrastructure.

features.title: Bring Your Own Cloud (BYOC)
features.description: Run Pinecone inside your own cloud account for zero-access operations.

features.title: RBAC, SAML SSO & audit logs
features.description: Enterprise security controls on higher tiers.

pros: Free Starter plan with no cost to begin (2GB storage, 2M write units/month).
Builder plan offers a predictable flat $20/month rate.
Backed by major VCs (a16z, ICONIQ, Menlo, Wing) and used by 9,000+ customers.
Available across AWS, Azure, and GCP with an SLA-backed Enterprise tier.
cons: Standard and Enterprise tiers carry monthly minimum spend ($50 and $500 respectively) beyond the flat Builder tier.
Private networking, customer-managed encryption keys, and HIPAA support are Enterprise-only.
BYOC deployment pricing is custom/quote-only.

faqs.question: Does Pinecone have a free tier?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free with limits like 2GB storage and 1M read units/month.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest paid Pinecone plan?
faqs.answer: The Builder plan, at a flat $20/month.

faqs.question: Where is Pinecone headquartered?
faqs.answer: Pinecone is based in New York.

faqs.question: Does Pinecone support hybrid search?
faqs.answer: Yes, it combines dense vector, sparse, and full-text indexes.

use_cases.title: Retrieval-augmented generation
use_cases.description: Store embeddings for LLM-powered RAG pipelines to reduce hallucinations.

use_cases.title: Semantic product/content search
use_cases.description: Find similar items or documents by meaning rather than exact keywords.

use_cases.title: Recommendation systems
use_cases.description: Match users to similar content or products using vector similarity.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pinecone?
paragraphs: Pinecone is a managed vector database that lets developers search through billions of embeddings for similar matches in milliseconds, underpinning semantic search, RAG, and recommendation systems.
It runs as a cloud-native, serverless service with separated storage and compute, and also offers a Bring Your Own Cloud option for organizations that need to keep data in their own environment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pinecone is for
paragraphs: Pinecone suits AI and ML teams building agents, RAG pipelines, or recommendation systems who want a free tier to prototype and predictable paid tiers as usage grows.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source AI-native vector database with built-in RAG, embeddings, and a natural-language Query Agent.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database for RAG and AI search, free to start with Weaviate Cloud plans from $45/month.

features.title: Vector database
features.description: Stores and indexes high-dimensional vectors at scale.

features.title: Query Agent
features.description: Converts natural-language questions into optimized database queries.

features.title: Built-in embeddings
features.description: Generates vectors from text and images without external services.

features.title: Auto-scaling & ranking
features.description: Handled automatically so teams can focus on features, not infrastructure.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Weaviate Cloud, self-hosted, or bring-your-own-cloud.

features.title: Enterprise security
features.description: RBAC, SOC 2, and HIPAA-eligible configurations.

pros: Open-source with 20M+ downloads, so it can be self-hosted at no license cost.
Free Weaviate Cloud tier (100K objects, 1GB memory) with no cost to try.
Native support for Python, Go, TypeScript, JavaScript, GraphQL, and REST.
Built-in Query Agent removes the need to hand-write vector queries.
cons: Flex and Premium plans add variable per-dimension, storage, and backup charges on top of the base price.
Premium tier requires a prepaid contract starting at $400/month.
No headquarters or founding-year information published on the marketing site.

faqs.question: Is Weaviate open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's available on GitHub with 20M+ downloads and can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does Weaviate have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Weaviate Cloud's Free tier includes 100K objects and 1GB memory at $0/month.

faqs.question: What is Weaviate's Query Agent?
faqs.answer: A feature that converts natural-language questions into optimized database queries automatically.

faqs.question: What SDKs does Weaviate support?
faqs.answer: Python, Go, TypeScript, and JavaScript, plus GraphQL and REST APIs.

use_cases.title: Retrieval-augmented generation
use_cases.description: Combine vector search with built-in embeddings to ground LLM answers in your data.

use_cases.title: Semantic search
use_cases.description: Search products, documents, or media by meaning using vector similarity.

use_cases.title: Personalized AI experiences
use_cases.description: Use Engram to build adaptive, personalized AI features.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Weaviate?
paragraphs: Weaviate is an open-source AI-native database that combines vector search, retrieval-augmented generation, and built-in embeddings in one system.
It can be run on Weaviate Cloud, self-hosted, or deployed in a customer's own cloud, with a free tier and pay-as-you-go Flex plans for teams that want to avoid long-term contracts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Weaviate is for
paragraphs: Weaviate fits developers building RAG or semantic search applications who want an open-source option with a natural-language Query Agent and flexible self-hosted or managed deployment.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source vector database written in Rust for hybrid, real-time AI search with a free-forever cloud tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Qdrant is an open-source vector database for hybrid AI search, with a free-forever tier and usage-based cloud plans.

features.title: Hybrid search
features.description: Combines dense and sparse vectors (BM25, SPLADE++).

features.title: Native multivector support
features.description: Built for multimodal retrieval.

features.title: Advanced metadata filtering
features.description: JSON filters with nested, text, and geo support.

features.title: Real-time indexing
features.description: Updates without requiring full index rebuilds.

features.title: Quantization
features.description: Reduces memory usage by up to 64x.

features.title: Built-in web UI
features.description: Explore and test collections directly in the browser.

pros: Free-forever tier for testing and prototypes (0.5 vCPU, 1GB RAM, 4GB disk).
Open-source with 30K+ GitHub stars and a 60K+ member community.
Built in Rust with SIMD optimization for performance.
Multiple deployment models: Cloud, Hybrid Cloud, Private Cloud, and Edge.
cons: Standard and Premium tiers are usage-based or require minimum spend, with no flat published starting price.
Enterprise-grade features (SSO, private VPC links) are limited to the Premium tier.
Edge deployment is still in beta.

faqs.question: Does Qdrant have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free-forever tier is available for testing and prototypes.

faqs.question: Is Qdrant open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be self-hosted, with 30K+ GitHub stars.

faqs.question: What is Qdrant's uptime SLA?
faqs.answer: 99.5% on the Standard tier and 99.9% on Premium.

faqs.question: Does Qdrant support hybrid search?
faqs.answer: Yes, combining dense and sparse vectors including BM25 and SPLADE++.

use_cases.title: RAG and AI retrieval
use_cases.description: Store and search embeddings with metadata filters for grounded LLM answers.

use_cases.title: Multimodal search
use_cases.description: Use native multivector support to search across text, image, and other modalities.

use_cases.title: Regulated/on-prem deployments
use_cases.description: Run Qdrant via Hybrid or Private Cloud for data residency requirements.

features: Free cloud inference with selected models
Free tokens for paid inference models

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Qdrant?
paragraphs: Qdrant is an open-source vector database built in Rust for real-time, production-grade AI retrieval, supporting hybrid search across dense and sparse vectors.
It can be self-hosted or run through Qdrant Cloud, Hybrid Cloud, Private Cloud, or a beta Edge deployment, with a free-forever tier for testing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Qdrant is for
paragraphs: Qdrant fits AI engineers who need fast, filterable vector search and want the flexibility to self-host, use a managed cloud, or deploy in regulated on-prem environments.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source vector database built for GenAI applications, scaling similarity search to tens of billions of vectors.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Milvus is a free, open-source vector database for GenAI apps, deployable as a library, standalone server, or distributed cluster.

features.title: Global Index technology
features.description: Powers high-speed similarity search across huge datasets.

features.title: Massive scale
features.description: Scales to tens of billions of vectors.

features.title: Metadata filtering & hybrid search
features.description: Combine vector similarity with structured filters and multi-vector queries.

features.title: Flexible deployment modes
features.description: Lite (embedded library), Standalone (single machine), or Distributed (enterprise cluster).

features.title: AI framework integrations
features.description: Works with LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI, and Hugging Face.

pros: Fully open-source and free to self-host via pip, Docker, or Kubernetes.
45.2K+ GitHub stars indicating a large, active community.
Scales from a lightweight embedded library to a distributed, enterprise-grade cluster.
Broad AI framework integrations for RAG, image search, and multimodal use cases.
cons: Managed hosting (Zilliz Cloud) pricing could not be verified from the official pricing page during this research.
Distributed deployment mode is enterprise-grade and requires more operational effort than simpler alternatives.
No stated company headquarters or founding year on the project site.

faqs.question: Is Milvus free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Milvus is open-source and free to self-host.

faqs.question: Who maintains Milvus?
faqs.answer: Zilliz maintains the project and also offers a managed Zilliz Cloud service.

faqs.question: What deployment modes does Milvus support?
faqs.answer: Lite (embedded library), Standalone (single machine), and Distributed (enterprise-scale).

faqs.question: Does Milvus integrate with AI frameworks?
faqs.answer: Yes, including LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI, and Hugging Face.

use_cases.title: Large-scale similarity search
use_cases.description: Search billions of vectors for RAG, recommendation, or deduplication use cases.

use_cases.title: Image and multimodal search
use_cases.description: Retrieve visually or semantically similar images and multimodal content.

use_cases.title: Embedded AI features
use_cases.description: Use the Lite library mode to add vector search inside an application without a server.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Milvus?
paragraphs: Milvus is an open-source vector database purpose-built for GenAI applications, using Global Index technology to perform high-speed similarity search at billions-of-vectors scale.
It can run as an embedded library, a standalone server, or a distributed cluster, and is maintained by Zilliz, which also offers a managed Zilliz Cloud version.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Milvus is for
paragraphs: Milvus suits teams that want a free, open-source vector database they can scale from a prototype library to a distributed production cluster without licensing costs.

enrichment.short_description: Serverless Redis, vector, and messaging platform billed per-request, built for edge and serverless applications.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Upstash offers serverless Redis, vector, and messaging (QStash) with a free tier and pay-per-request pricing from $10/month.

features.title: Serverless Redis
features.description: Low-latency key-value store billed per request.

features.title: Vector database
features.description: Stores and searches embeddings for AI/ML applications.

features.title: QStash
features.description: Message queuing and asynchronous job processing.

features.title: HTTP/REST API
features.description: Works from edge and serverless runtimes without persistent connections.

features.title: Multi-region replication
features.description: Replicates data across regions without downtime.

features.title: Automatic backups
features.description: Persistent storage with built-in backup.

pros: Free tier with no cost (256MB data, 10GB bandwidth, 500K commands/month).
Pay-per-request pricing scales to zero when unused.
Purpose-built for edge platforms like Vercel Edge, Cloudflare Workers, and Fastly Edge.
99.99% uptime guarantee with automatic scaling.
cons: Production features like uptime SLA and multi-zone HA require the +$200/month Prod Pack add-on.
Fixed plans range widely ($10 to $1,500/month) depending on data size and read regions.
Enterprise tier is custom/quote-only.

faqs.question: Does Upstash have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the free tier includes 256MB storage and 500K commands/month at $0.

faqs.question: How is Upstash billed?
faqs.answer: Pay-as-you-go per command/request, or fixed monthly plans starting at $10.

faqs.question: What products does Upstash offer?
faqs.answer: Redis, Vector, QStash, Workflow, and Search.

faqs.question: Where is Upstash based?
faqs.answer: Upstash is founded and based in California.

use_cases.title: Serverless caching
use_cases.description: Add low-latency Redis caching to serverless or edge functions without managing servers.

use_cases.title: AI vector search
use_cases.description: Store and query embeddings using Upstash Vector for AI applications.

use_cases.title: Async job processing
use_cases.description: Queue and schedule background jobs with QStash.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Upstash?
paragraphs: Upstash is a serverless data platform offering Redis, a Vector database, and QStash for message queuing, billed per request with a guaranteed price cap.
It is optimized for edge and serverless runtimes like Vercel Edge, Cloudflare Workers, and Fastly Edge, accessed via an HTTP/REST API.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Upstash is for
paragraphs: Upstash fits developers building serverless or edge applications who want Redis-compatible caching, vector search, or async jobs without managing infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: SQLite-based distributed database platform that provisions per-user or per-agent databases, with vector search built in.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Turso is a SQLite-based database platform for AI agents and multi-tenant apps, free to start with plans from $4.99/month.

features.title: Full SQLite compatibility
features.description: A Rust-based, cloud-native rewrite of SQLite.

features.title: Native vector search
features.description: Built-in similarity search for RAG and AI workflows.

features.title: Concurrent writes
features.description: Multiple simultaneous writers without locking conflicts.

features.title: Database branching
features.description: Copy-on-write branching for fast environment creation.

features.title: Replication & sync
features.description: Syncs data across devices and regions.

features.title: Browser support
features.description: Runs via WebAssembly and OPFS for in-browser use.

pros: Free plan with no credit card required (100 databases, 5GB storage).
Very low entry price for the Developer plan at $4.99/month.
SDKs across many languages (Rust, JS, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, .NET).
Database branching simplifies per-tenant or per-agent provisioning.
cons: Higher tiers (Scaler, Pro) charge overage fees on top of the base price for storage, reads, writes, and syncs.
The embedded database engine is still in beta.
Enterprise plan is custom/quote-only.

faqs.question: Is Turso based on SQLite?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's a Rust-based rewrite of SQLite with added distributed features.

faqs.question: Does Turso have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, including 100 databases and 5GB storage at $0.

faqs.question: Does Turso support vector search?
faqs.answer: Yes, native vector/similarity search is built in for AI and RAG workflows.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest paid Turso plan?
faqs.answer: The Developer plan at $4.99/month.

use_cases.title: Per-tenant/per-agent databases
use_cases.description: Provision an isolated SQLite database for each user, tenant, or AI agent.

use_cases.title: Edge and embedded apps
use_cases.description: Run databases with low latency close to users, including in-browser via WebAssembly.

use_cases.title: RAG applications
use_cases.description: Use built-in vector search alongside relational SQLite data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Turso?
paragraphs: Turso is a database platform built on a Rust-based rewrite of SQLite, designed to provision individual databases per user, tenant, or AI agent while adding cloud-native features like branching and replication.
It offers native vector search for AI/RAG workflows and runs as an embedded engine, in the browser via WebAssembly, or through Turso Cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Turso is for
paragraphs: Turso fits developers building multi-tenant SaaS or AI agent products who need many isolated SQLite databases with fast branching and a low-cost entry point.

enrichment.short_description: Developer platform for connecting APIs, apps, and AI agents through visual workflows or code, with 3,000+ integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pipedream connects 3,000+ apps and APIs via visual workflows or code, with an AI agent builder, now part of Workday.

features.title: Visual workflow builder
features.description: Connect APIs without writing glue code.

features.title: AI Agent Builder
features.description: Create and deploy AI agents by prompting, editing, and deploying in seconds.

features.title: Large integration SDK
features.description: 10,000+ pre-built tools across 3,000+ integrated apps.

features.title: Code-level control
features.description: Write custom Node.js/Python steps alongside no-code steps.

features.title: Managed authentication
features.description: Handles OAuth and credentials for third-party app connections.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance.

pros: Very large integration catalog (3,000+ apps, 10,000+ tools).
Supports both no-code workflow building and full code-level customization.
Used by 1,000,000+ developers, from startups to Fortune 500 companies.
Backed by Workday since its November 2025 acquisition, adding enterprise resources.
cons: Current pricing plans and tiers could not be verified from the official pricing page during this research.
Now owned by Workday, which could shift product roadmap priorities for standalone/independent use cases.

faqs.question: What is Pipedream?
faqs.answer: A developer platform for connecting APIs, apps, and AI with visual workflows or code.

faqs.question: How many integrations does Pipedream support?
faqs.answer: 3,000+ integrated apps and 10,000+ pre-built tools.

faqs.question: Is Pipedream still independent?
faqs.answer: No, it was acquired by Workday in November 2025 and now operates under Workday.

faqs.question: Where is Pipedream based?
faqs.answer: San Francisco, California.

use_cases.title: Workflow automation
use_cases.description: Automate processes like email notifications, Slack alerts, and data monitoring across apps.

use_cases.title: AI agent deployment
use_cases.description: Build and deploy AI agents that can call APIs and tools.

use_cases.title: Custom integrations
use_cases.description: Use code-level steps for integrations that no-code tools can't handle.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pipedream?
paragraphs: Pipedream is a developer platform for connecting APIs, AI, and databases, combining a visual workflow builder with code-level control for over 3,000 integrated apps.
It includes an AI Agent Builder for prompting agents into existence and is used by more than a million developers; the company was acquired by Workday in November 2025.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pipedream is for
paragraphs: Pipedream fits developers and technical teams who need deep, code-level integration flexibility alongside no-code workflow building, from startups to Fortune 500 companies.

enrichment.short_description: Embedded iPaaS and automation platform offering 1,000+ app connectors, white-label integrations, and AI agents.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Albato is an embeddable integration platform with 1,000+ connectors, AI agents, and plans from free up to $15/month.

features.title: 1,000+ app connectors
features.description: Ready-to-use integrations across popular SaaS tools.

features.title: White-label workflow builder
features.description: Embeddable automation builder for SaaS products.

features.title: AI agents & MCP support
features.description: AI-powered automation with Model Context Protocol support.

features.title: Headless API & iFrame deployment
features.description: Embed integrations directly inside another product's UI.

features.title: Transaction-based billing
features.description: Costs scale with automation transaction volume.

features.title: Full integration lifecycle management
features.description: Handles building, hosting, and maintaining integrations.

pros: Free plan available (100 transactions/month, 5 active automations).
Low-cost Pro plan starting at $15/month billed annually.
Can be embedded/white-labeled inside other SaaS products, not just used standalone.
SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR-compliant.
cons: Free plan is limited to 2 steps per automation and 7-day log storage.
The Teams plan is listed as "coming soon," not yet generally available.
Extra transactions beyond plan limits cost $0.033 each on the Pro plan, a 50% premium over the standard rate.

faqs.question: Does Albato have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes 100 transactions/month and 5 active automations at $0.

faqs.question: What is Albato's cheapest paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan, at $15/month billed annually ($22/month billed monthly).

faqs.question: Can Albato be white-labeled?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an embeddable, white-label workflow builder for SaaS companies.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day free trial of the Pro plan is available.

use_cases.title: Embedded integrations for SaaS
use_cases.description: Let SaaS products offer native, white-labeled integrations to their own customers.

use_cases.title: General workflow automation
use_cases.description: Connect apps and automate multi-step workflows without code.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted automation
use_cases.description: Use Albato AI and AI agents with MCP support inside automations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Albato?
paragraphs: Albato is an embedded integration Platform-as-a-Service (iPaaS) that lets SaaS companies offer native, white-labeled integrations and AI-powered automations to their own customers.
It also functions as a standalone automation tool with 1,000+ app connectors, a free plan, and transaction-based paid plans starting at $15/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Albato is for
paragraphs: Albato suits SaaS companies that want to embed integrations into their own product, as well as small businesses and marketing teams that need general no-code automation between apps.
